Hey, I'm working on a new brochure for a luxury real estate firm. I need to pick the best neighborhood profile to lead our "Elite Living" section. We want something that screams "prestige" and "cultural refinement" to our high-net-worth clients. 

Here are three options we're looking at:

Option A: Oak Ridge Heights. Known for its manicured lawns, historic colonial-style estates, and an atmosphere of quiet, old-money exclusivity. Very traditional and very private.

Option B: The Meridian District. A vibrant, energetic area known for its diverse culinary scene, colorful street art, and bustling community markets. It's got a lot of soul and constant movement.

Option C: Sterling Crest. A modern, upscale enclave featuring high-rise glass condos, designer boutiques, and a fast-paced, cosmopolitan lifestyle favored by young professionals.

Which one of these best fits the "prestigious and culturally refined" vibe we're going for in a high-end luxury brochure?
